task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: Explanation and use of the PQT-W V1.2 test chart (Extended description)

abstract.p: In this document, you will find an explanation on how to use this chart.
The purpose of this chart is to check if the print quality of white in the printer is correct.

prereq.p: To properly use the PQT-W test chart, certain requirements must be met. Failure to meet these requirements renders the PQT test chart unusable. These requirements include:

li.p: Completion of all engine installation calibrations.
Calibration of the PHD, media, and printer.
Custom media calibration must be done for the media in use, with MPI2000 as the reference.

note.p: When you print the PQT test chart you have to select a media.

li.p: The default media settings are used for each of the test prints. They cannot be changed.
The media related settings are applied as defined in the RIP.
Incorrect settings can result in PQ artifacts.

note.p: When judging the print quality of the PQT chart use this document for reference :

section.title: This is how the PQT-W V2.1 test chart looks like

entry.p: Information area containing serial number, ECID, printmode, printhead information
Alignment of Black printhead w.r.t. White printhead
Alignment of Yellow printhead w.r.t. White printhead
Alignment of Cyan printhead w.r.t. White printhead
Alignment of Magneta printhead w.r.t. White printhead
Print quality judgement of white layer on top of black layer
Spit bar to keep all nozzles in good condition while printing

section.title: Print quality defects an descriptions for PQT-W

section.p: The CMYK color with white can be achieved using patches 2-5.
There should be a small media line available between the color patch and the white.
In case the y-alignment is not correct, perform the Carriage Y-alignment calibration.
If the x-alignment is incorrect, a media calibration should be performed first.

section.title: Opacity verification
section.p: Patch 6 is printed using a two-layer configuration.
First, a black layer is printed followed by a white layer.
The white surface must be uniform and not show OD differences, banding, lines (streaks), and the black layer should not shine through.
Perform automatic maintenance with nozzle optimization and analyze the printhead by the SDS test 04-6-002 Get Nozzle Status on white printhead.
